diagrams.title: Static vs Dynamic Scheduling Distribution
diagrams.description: Comparison showing how 16 iterations are distributed among 4 threads using different scheduling strategies
elements: Static: Thread0=0-3, Thread1=4-7, Thread2=8-11, Thread3=12-15 (predetermined at compile time)
Dynamic(4): Chunks of 4 assigned as threads complete work
Guided: Starts with chunk=4, decreases to smaller chunks as work remaining decreases
Dynamic(1): Each thread gets 1 iteration at a time, maximum flexibility
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how different scheduling policies distribute work across threads and understand when each is appropriate

diagrams.title: Guided Scheduling Chunk Size Decrease
diagrams.description: Graph showing how chunk size decreases over time in guided scheduling as remaining iterations decrease
elements: Curve: Starts at ~N/p (N=iterations, p=threads), decreases following max(chunk, remaining/p)
Shows how early chunks are large (efficient), late chunks small (balanced)
diagrams.purpose: Understand the adaptive nature of guided scheduling and its efficiency-load balance trade-off

diagrams.title: Scheduling Overhead vs Load Balance Trade-off
diagrams.description: Visual representation of the spectrum from static to dynamic scheduling showing overhead and balance characteristics
elements: Static (left): Lowest overhead, worst balance for variable loads
Dynamic(small chunks) (right): Highest overhead, best balance
Arrow indicating optimal point depends on workload characteristics
Shows chunk size as control variable affecting both axes
diagrams.purpose: Help choose appropriate scheduling strategy based on workload characteristics and performance requirements
